flex-direction: column;"><div><div><div class="ArticleHeader_main_wrapper__yCL1Y" style="display: flex; align-items: center;"><div style="width: 100%;"><div class="ArticleHeader_article-title__futDC"><h1>Painter's Algorithm in Computer Graphics</h1></div><div class="ArticleHeader_last_updated_parent__ohhpb"><div><span>Last Updated : </span><span>18 Jun, 2024</span></div><div class="ArticleHeader_last_updated_parent--three_dot_dropdown__yslcL"><div styles="[object Object]"><div class="ArticleThreeDot_threedotcontainer__dfGWD"><div class="ArticleThreeDot_comment__gJfFl" style="background-position: 0px 0px;"></div><div class="ArticleThreeDot_improve___NFbl" style="background-position: -40px -521px;"></div><div class="ArticleThreeDot_threedot-wrapper__q4ELY"><ul class="ArticleThreeDot_threedot__ZQ19q"><li></li><li></li><li></li></ul></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="MainArticleContent_articleMainContentCss__b_1_R article--viewer_content"><div class="a-wrapper"><div class="content"><div class="text"><div class="html-chunk"><p dir="ltr"><b><strong>Painter's algorithm </strong></b><span>is the algorithm which is introduced by </span><b><strong>Hewells </strong></b><span> in </span><b><strong>1972</strong></b><span>.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>The techniques used by these algorithms are</span><b><strong> image space </strong></b><span>and </span><b><strong>object space</strong></b><span>.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>The name of this algorithm is Painter's because it's working is like a painter who creating an </span><b><strong>oil painting</strong></b><span>. Just like an artist paints, he start his painting with an empty canvas, the first thing the artist will do is to create a </span><b><strong>background layer</strong></b><span> for the painting, after this layer he start creating </span><b><strong>another layers</strong></b><span> of objects</span><b><strong> one-by-one</strong></b><span>. In this way he completes his painting, by covering the previous layer partially or fully according to the requirement of the painting.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>This algorithm is basically used to paint the </span><b><strong>polygons</strong></b><span> in the view plane by considering their distance from the viewer. The polygons which are at more distance from the viewer are painted first. After that the nearer polygons are started painted on or over more distant polygons according to the requirement.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>In this algorithm the polygons or surfaces in the scene are firstly scanned or then painted in the frame buffer in the </span><b><strong>decreasing distance</strong></b><span> from view point of the viewer starting with the polygons of </span><b><strong>maximum depth</strong></b><span> or we can say minimum z-value.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>Firstly the </span><b><strong>depth sort</strong></b><span> is performed in which the polygons are listed according to their </span><b><strong>visibility order</strong></b><span> or depth priority.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>As this algorithm uses the concept of depth priority so it is also called as </span><b><strong>depth priority algorithm</strong></b><span> or </span><b><strong>priority algorithm</strong></b><span>.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>The frame buffer is painted with the background color. After that the polygon which is farthest enter to the frame buffer. For this, the pixel information will get changed i.e. information of the background which has the farthest polygon get replaced with that of the background. This is going to be repeatedly changed as we move from one polygon to the other and end up with the nearest polygon.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>Usually</span><b><strong> comparisons</strong></b><span> are performed whenever the polygons are going to overlap each other. The most common method used for the comparison is called as </span><b><strong>mini-max method</strong></b><span>. For this purpose, the rectangles are drawn around the polygons such that the rectangles exactly fit the polygons.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>Then the rectangles are going to check to see whether they overlap each other or not. If the rectangles are observed as they do not overlap then we consider that the surfaces are also not overlap. If the rectangles are overlapped then the surfaces are also overlapped which is as shown in the following figure:</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><img height="inherit" src="https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20200702091247/rectangles.jpg" width="inherit" title="Click to enlarge" style="cursor: zoom-in;"></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>To find out which rectangle is to be overlap, we need to find the minimum and maximum x and y values to the rectangles which are going to test for overlapping. If the minimum value of y of one of the rectangles is larger than the maximum y value of the other rectangle then they are not going to be overlapped and as the rectangles are not overlapped then the surfaces are also not overlapped.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>The same test is to be performed for the x-coordinates.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><span>If the surfaces are overlapping, we do not know which surface should be present on the top of the other. To find out which surface is to be present on the top the </span><b><strong>principle of</strong></b><span> </span><b><strong>mini-max </strong></b><span>is used on the depth values of both the overlapping surfaces.</span></p>
<p dir="ltr"><b><strong>Example:</strong></b></p>

<p></p><figure class="image"><img alt="imagesjpg-copy" height="492" loading="lazy" src="https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy.webp" srcset="https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy.webp 800w,https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy-100.webp 100w,https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy-200.webp 200w,https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy-300.webp 300w,https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy-660.webp 660w,https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20240618110243/imagesjpg-copy-768.webp 768w" width="800" title="Click to enlarge" style="cursor: zoom-in;"><figcaption></figcaption></figure>

<h3><span>Algorithm for Painter's method</span></h3><p dir="ltr"><span>The following are the steps of this algorithm:</span></p>
<ol><li value="1"><span>Sorting of the various surfaces which is on the basis of their decreasing depth or we can say the largest value of z.</span></li><li value="2"><span>Now scanning to convert the various surfaces which is in the order starting with the surface which has greatest depth.</span></li><li value="3"><span>Comparing is to be done on the basis of various overlapping surfaces so that the user will determine which surface is to be kept visible.</span></li><li value="4"><span>In the refresh buffer enter the intensity value for the determined surface i.e. the surface which is determined to be visible.</span></li><li value="5"><span>The above process is going to be repeat for all the available surfaces.</span></li><li value="6"><span>However, if the overlapping is observed, then there is the need of the further tests. space\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe name of this algorithm is Painter's because it's working is like a painter who creating an \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eoil painting\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. Just like an artist paints, he start his painting with an empty canvas, the first thing the artist will do is to create a \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ebackground layer\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e for the painting, after this layer he start creating \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eanother layers\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e of objects\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e one-by-one\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. In this way he completes his painting, by covering the previous layer partially or fully according to the requirement of the painting.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThis algorithm is basically used to paint the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epolygons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e in the view plane by considering their distance from the viewer. The polygons which are at more distance from the viewer are painted first. After that the nearer polygons are started painted on or over more distant polygons according to the requirement.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eIn this algorithm the polygons or surfaces in the scene are firstly scanned or then painted in the frame buffer in the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edecreasing distance\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e from view point of the viewer starting with the polygons of \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003emaximum depth\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or we can say minimum z-value.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eFirstly the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth sort\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e is performed in which the polygons are listed according to their \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003evisibility order\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or depth priority.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eAs this algorithm uses the concept of depth priority so it is also called as \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth priority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epriority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe frame buffer is painted with the background color. After that the polygon which is farthest enter to the frame buffer. For this, the pixel information will get changed i.e. information of the background which has the farthest polygon get replaced with that of the background. This is going to be repeatedly changed as we move from one polygon to the other and end up with the nearest polygon.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eUsually\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e comparisons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e are performed whenever the polygons are going to overlap each other. The most common method used for the comparison is called as \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003emini-max method\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. For this purpose, the rectangles are drawn around the polygons such that the rectangles exactly fit the polygons.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThen the rectangles are going to check to see whether they overlap each other or not. If the rectangles are overlapped then the surfaces are also overlapped which is as shown in the following figure:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg height=\"inherit\" src=\"https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20200702091247/rectangles.jpg\" width=\"inherit\"/\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eTo find out which rectangle is to be overlap, we need to find the minimum and maximum x and y values to the rectangles which are going to test for overlapping. Just like an artist paints, he start his painting with an empty canvas, the first thing the artist will do is to create a \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ebackground layer\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e for the painting, after this layer he start creating \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eanother layers\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e of objects\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e one-by-one\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. In this way he completes his painting, by covering the previous layer partially or fully according to the requirement of the painting.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThis algorithm is basically used to paint the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epolygons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e in the view plane by considering their distance from the viewer. The polygons which are at more distance from the viewer are painted first. After that the nearer polygons are started painted on or over more distant polygons according to the requirement.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eIn this algorithm the polygons or surfaces in the scene are firstly scanned or then painted in the frame buffer in the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edecreasing distance\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e from view point of the viewer starting with the polygons of \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003emaximum depth\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or we can say minimum z-value.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eFirstly the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth sort\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e is performed in which the polygons are listed according to their \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003evisibility order\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or depth priority.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eAs this algorithm uses the concept of depth priority so it is also called as \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth priority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epriority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe frame buffer is painted with the background color. After that the polygon which is farthest enter to the frame buffer. For this, the pixel information will get changed i.e. information of the background which has the farthest polygon get replaced with that of the background. This is going to be repeatedly changed as we move from one polygon to the other and end up with the nearest polygon.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eUsually\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e comparisons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e are performed whenever the polygons are going to overlap each other. dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePainter's algorithm \u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003eis the algorithm which is introduced by \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHewells \u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e in \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e1972\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe techniques used by these algorithms are\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e image space \u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003eand \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eobject space\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe name of this algorithm is Painter's because it's working is like a painter who creating an \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eoil painting\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. Just like an artist paints, he start his painting with an empty canvas, the first thing the artist will do is to create a \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ebackground layer\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e for the painting, after this layer he start creating \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eanother layers\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e of objects\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e one-by-one\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. In this way he completes his painting, by covering the previous layer partially or fully according to the requirement of the painting.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThis algorithm is basically used to paint the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epolygons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e in the view plane by considering their distance from the viewer. The polygons which are at more distance from the viewer are painted first. After that the nearer polygons are started painted on or over more distant polygons according to the requirement.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eIn this algorithm the polygons or surfaces in the scene are firstly scanned or then painted in the frame buffer in the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edecreasing distance\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e from view point of the viewer starting with the polygons of \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003emaximum depth\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or we can say minimum z-value.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eFirstly the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth sort\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e is performed in which the polygons are listed according to their \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003evisibility order\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or depth priority.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eAs this algorithm uses the concept of depth priority so it is also called as \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edepth priority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e or \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003epriority algorithm\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe frame buffer is painted with the background color. After that the polygon which is farthest enter to the frame buffer. For this, the pixel information will get changed i.e. information of the background which has the farthest polygon get replaced with that of the background. This is going to be repeatedly changed as we move from one polygon to the other and end up with the nearest polygon.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eUsually\u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e comparisons\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e are performed whenever the polygons are going to overlap each other. The most common method used for the comparison is called as \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003emini-max method\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e. For this purpose, the rectangles are drawn around the polygons such that the rectangles exactly fit the polygons.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThen the rectangles are going to check to see whether they overlap each other or not. If the rectangles are observed as they do not overlap then we consider that the surfaces are also not overlap. If the rectangles are overlapped then the surfaces are also overlapped which is as shown in the following figure:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg height=\"inherit\" src=\"https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20200702091247/rectangles.jpg\" width=\"inherit\"/\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eTo find out which rectangle is to be overlap, we need to find the minimum and maximum x and y values to the rectangles which are going to test for overlapping. If the minimum value of y of one of the rectangles is larger than the maximum y value of the other rectangle then they are not going to be overlapped and as the rectangles are not overlapped then the surfaces are also not overlapped.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThe same test is to be performed for the x-coordinates.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan\u003eIf the surfaces are overlapping, we do not know which surface should be present on the top of the other. To find out which surface is to be present on the top the \u003c/span\u003e\u003cb\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eprinciple of\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan\u003e
